info.title: boomApp Connect
info.description: Boost customer engagement with Boomerang’s SMS, Voice, and Email Connector for the Microsoft Power Platform. Deliver automated alerts, reminders, and two-way conversational messaging directly from your Microsoft applications. Leverage real-time responses to streamline and automate communication workflows. With advanced inbound messaging features, customers can initiate conversations that integrate instantly into your business processes, driving faster, more efficient interactions.

tags.name: Responses
tags.description: Retrieve message responses and delivery receipts

post.description:
SMS messages are delivered using an alpha-numeric originator (such as a company name) which must contain a maximum of 11 characters. A recipient cannot respond to a message sent using an alpha-numeric originator. Please also note that in destinations where delivery of messages with alpha-numeric originator is not supported (due to local network policies), this may be replaced with a numeric originator, to ensure that the message is delivered.

**Note regarding use of Social Hours**: Social Hours ensures messages are processed between designated times, regardless of when the request is received. [See SMS Delivery Infrastructure](https://boomerangmessaging.halocrm.io/kb?id=33).  A default configuration for Social Hours can be assigned to your Boomerang account or passed in each request using the 'social_hours' parameters.  Please note that a Social Hours subscription is required for these options to be enabled.

post.description:
This method allows replies to be tracked directly against each outbound transaction and is used to manage conversations between your applications and the end user. Replies can be forwarded to an email address and customers with Boommail can send an email-2-SMS message back to the responder. [See SMS Delivery Infrastructure](https://boomerangmessaging.halocrm.io/kb?id=33), for more information regarding 2-way and conversational messaging.

**Note regarding use of Social Hours**: Social Hours ensures messages are processed between designated times, regardless of when the request is received.  A default configuration for Social Hours can be assigned to your Boomerang account or passed in each request using the 'social_hours' parameters.  Please note that a Social Hours subscription is required for these options to be enabled.

post.description: SMS messages are delivered from an originating number specified in the request so that any replies are automatically returned to that number. Customers using Boomerang's Inbound Campaign feature can use their inbound campaign number as the originator for outbound broadcasts, so that any replies returned to the number can be retrieved using the get-Inbound function. All inbound messages are also accessible via your Boomerang UI account. Unlike the SMS2 function, replies are not tracked against the outbound message. For more information regarding SMS messaging, [See SMS Delivery Infrastructure](https://boomerangmessaging.halocrm.io/kb?id=33).

post.description: Send Text-To-Speech (TTS) messages delivered as a voice call. On answering the call, a greeting / welcome message is played, allowing recipients to confirm that they wish to hear the main message.  Replies are returned as a keystroke via the device keypad. Please see [See Voice Delivery Infrastructure](https://boomerangmessaging.halocrm.io/kb?id=37) for more information.

get.description: This method allows a response or responses (where open ticket is applied) to an outbound transaction to be retrieved based on criteria associated to the outbound messages.

parameters.name: unique_identifier
parameters.description: Returns all responses associated to a single outbound transaction based on the value passed in the unique_identifier parameter when sending a message

parameters.name: custom_parameter
parameters.description: Returns all responses associated to a any outbound messages sent with the corresponding value passed in custom_parameter when sending a message.

parameters.name: campaign_name
parameters.description: Returns all responses associated to a any outbound messages sent with the corresponding value passed in campaign_name when sending a message.

parameters.name: transaction_id
parameters.description: Returns all responses associated to a single outbound transaction based on the transaction Id returned the response when sending an outbound message.

parameters.name: ignore_previous
parameters.description: Pass 'true' to exclude any responses that have been retrieved previously and marked as read.

parameters.name: mark_as_read
parameters.description: Pass 'true' to flag responses retrieved in a request as read. Passing 'true' against ignore_previous will ensure that only new responses are returned. Passing 'false' will include previously retrieved responses. Where 'false' is passed this will override ignore_previous = true as all responses will be treated as new.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Where the paramter has_more = true, pass the relevant page number to return the more responses.

parameters.name: conversationId
parameters.description: Returns all responses associated to a conversation thread based on the value passed in conversation_id parameter when sending a two-way message. Pass the corresponding value to retrieve responses associated to that thread.

get.summary: Retrieve responses to outbound messages (Legacy alias)

get.summary: Retrieve delivery status updates for outbound messages
get.description: This function is used to retrieve delivery status updates returned by the recipient’s device or service provider which can be used to update the status of the corresponding outbound message. Refer to our [Knowledge Articles](https://boomerangmessaging.halocrm.io/kb?id=33) for further information on delivery statuses for each communication channel.

parameters.name: drs_after
parameters.description: Provide the date and time after which delivery receipts should be returned. This parameter is also mandatory when using start_transaction_id and will return all DRs after the time specified.

parameters.name: start_transaction_id
parameters.description: Delivery status updates will be returned for all transaction Ids including and subsequent to the Id provided

parameters.name: ignore_previous
parameters.description: Pass 'true' to exclude any DRs that have been retrieved previously.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pass the required page number where more DRs are available.

400.description: Your request could not be processed

401.description: Your request could not be authenticated

500.description: Please try again or report this error to the support

get.description: Triggered by delivery status updates returned. Updates the transaction with the delivery status returned by the end user's network operator. Refer to our [Knowledge Articles](https://boomerangmessaging.halocrm.io/kb?id=33) for further information on delivery statuses for each communication channel.

parameters.name: drs_after
parameters.description: Provide the date and time after which delivery receipts should be returned. This parameter is also mandatory when using start_transaction_id and will return all DRs after the time specified.

parameters.name: start_transaction_id
parameters.description: Delivery status updates will be returned for all transaction Ids including and subsequent to the Id provided

parameters.name: ignore_previous
parameters.description: Pass 'true' to exclude any DRs that have been retrieved previously.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pass the required page number where more DRs are available.

get.description: Retrieve messages submitted to an inbound campaign number.  Inbound campaign numbers allow end users to initiate engagement with your organisation via SMS and the inbound message can then be used as a trigger to initiate a resulting workflow. End users could for example, request an appointment which could trigger a flow that sends a follow up SMS containing the next available slots. Additionally, using a campaign number as the originating number when calling the SMS3 (custom number) function, allows you to initiate a broadcast to your stakeholders and with any replies being automatically returned to the inbound campaign number. Inbound campaign numbers can be purchased via your Boomerang UI account or on request by sending an email to operations@boomcomms.com.

parameters.name: after_ref
parameters.description: Returns all inbound messages on or after the inbound id provided.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Returns all messages on or after the date and time provided.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Returns all messages on or before the date and time provided.

parameters.name: to_inbound_number
parameters.description: Returns all messages submitted to the number provided.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Returns all messages submitted by the end user number provided.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of messages to return per page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number to return.

get.description: Non-trigger endpoint to retrieve messages submitted to an inbound campaign number (same parameters/shape as the trigger endpoint, but without x-ms-trigger metadata).

post.description: Method providing the ability to set-up 2-way conversational messaging between two different parties, without either disclosing their personal contact details. Once a conversation is initiated, the personal details of both parties are automatically masked across a specified number of interactions, after which, the recipient’s contact details are disclosed to the initiator. Where the number of interactions is not specified, a conversation will be masked across an unlimited number of interactions, without any personal details ever being disclosed.

from.description: Sender Id displayed in the recipient's device. An alpha-numeric value containing a maximum of 11 characters. Note, a recipient is unable to reply, if replies are required, use the SMS two-way function.

message_content.description: The content of the outbound message. Up to 3000 characters supported. Multi-part messages are concatenated and delivered as a single message

priority.description: Optional parameter used to override ‘Social Hours’ settings if enabled in your Boomerang account. Pass ‘true’ to override Social Hours and messages are processed immediately on submission.

unique_identifier.description: Unique identifier for each individual message transaction. This is used to safeguard against duplication of messages, as Boomerang will ignore any transactions with an Id that has been used previously.

campaign_name.description: Optional parameter that can be used to organise messages by specific campaigns.

custom_parameter.description: Optional parameter used for a custom reference, for example a job reference, use case, batch Id etc.

delay.description: Pass a value in minutes to defer processing of a request by the period specified.

social_hours_monday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_monday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_tuesday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_tuesday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_tuesday_day_off.description: Pass ‘true’ to disable Social Hours for this day.

social_hours_wednesday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_wednesday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_thursday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_thursday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_friday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_friday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_saturday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_saturday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_sunday_start.description: Define the start time for Social Hours.

social_hours_sunday_end.description: Define the end time for Social Hours.

social_hours_USA_state.description: Localise Social Hours when sending to recipients in the USA.

social_hours_offset.description: Localise Social Hours to the recipient, based on an offset period set against UTC.

social_hours_timezone.description: Localise Social Hours to the recipient based on a specific time zone.

social_hours_from_destination.description: Localise Social Hours to the recipient based on the country prefix / dialling code contained in the recipient's telephone number.

shortlinks_enabled.description: Pass true to enable use of short-links.

shortlinks_password.description: Password protect access to the short-link.

shortlinks_expires.description: Mandatory parameter when short links are enabled.

shortlinks_starting_with.description: Domain(s) associated to URL(s) in your message content.

conversation_id.description: Parameter used to manage conversation threads.

quarantined_days.description: Quarantine period for originating number reuse.

message_content.description: The content of the outbound message.

from.description: A numeric sending Id associated to a message.

social_hours_saturday_day_off.description: Disable Social Hours for this day.

social_hours_sunday_day_off.description: Disable Social Hours for this day.

from.description: The sender id associated to the email, which is displayed in the recipient’s Inbox. If left blank, a default value will be taken from your Boomerang account settings.

email_subject.description: The subject associated to the email message, displayed in the recipient’s Inbox

message_content.description: The content of the Email body. There are no character limits and HTML content is supported.

email_address.description: Recipient Email address. A single address or an array of up to 10,000 addresses can be passed.

email_responses.description: An email address to which message responses are forwarded.

push_responses.description: A CallBack URL to which message responses are posted.

unique_identifier.description: A unique identifier for each individual message transaction provided by the customer to safeguard against duplication of messages.

campaign_name.description: Optional parameter that can be used to organise messages by specific campaigns.

custom_parameter.description: Optional parameter used for a custom reference, for example a job reference, use case, batch Id etc.

shortlinks_enabled.description: Pass true to enable use of short-links.

shortlinks_password.description: Password protect access to the short-link.

shortlinks_expires.description: Mandatory parameter when short links are enabled. Pass a date and time in UTC.

shortlinks_starting_with.description: Domain(s) associated to URL(s) in your message content.

voice_intro.example: Press 1 to accept or 2 to ignore.
voice_intro.description: The welcome message played when the voice call is answered.

voice_thank_you.description: The exit message played after the recipient has listened to, or responded to the main message.

voice_redirect_message.description: Optional. The message played when a call is being diverted.

message_content.description: Main message body played after intro confirmation.

email_responses.description: Email address to forward response details.

locale.description: Voice locale/accent/language. See supported locales list.

unique_identifier.name: unique_identifier
unique_identifier.description: Returns all responses associated to a single outbound transaction based on unique_identifier.

custom_parameter.name: custom_parameter
custom_parameter.description: Returns responses associated to outbound messages with custom_parameter.

campaign_name.name: campaign_name
campaign_name.description: Returns responses associated to outbound messages with campaign_name.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.description: Returns responses for a single outbound transaction based on transaction_id.

conversationId.name: conversationId
conversationId.description: Retrieve responses for a conversation thread.

start_transaction_id.name: start_transaction_id
start_transaction_id.description: Return delivery receipts from this transaction id onwards.

after_ref.name: after_ref
after_ref.description: Returns inbound messages on or after inbound ref.

after.name: after
after.description: Returns inbound messages on or after datetime.

before.name: before
before.description: Returns inbound messages on or before datetime.
